question_text: Let $ A = \left[ a \quad u _ { 1 } \quad u _ { 2 } \quad u _ { 3 } \right] , \quad B = \left[ b \quad u _ { 1 } \quad u _ { 2 } \quad u _ { 3 } \right] , \quad C = \left[ u _ { 2 } \quad u _ { 3 } \quad u _ { 1 } \quad a + b \right] . $ Let det(A), det(B), det(C) denote the determinants of the matrices A, B, and $C ,$ respectively. If $ \operatorname * { d e t } ( A ) = 6 , \quad \operatorname * { d e t } ( B ) = 2 , \quad \operatorname { t h e n } \ \operatorname * { d e t } ( A + B ) - \operatorname * { d e t } ( C ) = . . . . . . . . . . $
solution:
Given the determinants of A and B, we can use properties of determinants to simplify the expression det $( A + B ) - \operatorname* { d e t } ( C )$ . Calculating gives:

 $ \operatorname* { d e t } ( A + B ) - \operatorname* { d e t } ( C ) = 7 . $ 

Thus, the value is 7.
Quick Tip

When working with determinants of sums, use properties of determinants and matrix operations to simplify the calculations.
